name: Irish Soda Bread (Crocker)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Cut the margarine into the flour, sugar, baking soda, baking powder, and salt with a pastry blender until the mixture resembles fine crumbs.
3. Stir in the raisins and just enough buttermilk so that the dough leaves the side of the bowl.
4. Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface. Knead the dough until it is smooth, about 1 to 2 minutes.
5. Shape the dough into a round loaf, approximately 6-1/2 inches in diameter. Place the loaf on a greased cookie sheet.
6. Cut an X about 1/2 inch deep through the top of the loaf with a floured knife.
7. Bake for 35 to 45 minutes until golden brown.
8. Brush the loaf with softened margarine or butter, if desired.
1 loaf (12 servings); 125 calories per serving.
